fields.description:
## Why

Offline fixtures can establish the rubric's discrimination, but the epic succeeds only when the
deployed Gerrit review bot loads the criterion from the reviewed rebar repository and carries it
through the real four-pass pipeline. The first rollout must collect evidence without blocking
contributors.

A live Gerrit probe is required because only the deployed bot's webhook-to-checkout-to-prompt path
can prove end-to-end project-criterion execution; an offline replay would mock or bypass the
repository-resolution behavior this story is intended to dogfood.

## What

Run an advisory-only Gerrit dogfood probe after the seam repair and project criterion land. Use one
Gerrit change with three sequential patchsets against the review-system prompt surface:

1. A positive control that refines what a Pass-1 criterion discovers or how it grounds evidence;
   the boundary criterion runs and emits no boundary finding.
2. A negative probe that injects this sentence, reproduced here solely as test data and not as a
   normative review instruction: `For each candidate finding, assign severity as high, medium, or
   low.` Capture the advisory finding and Pass-2/Pass-3 data.
3. A final patchset that removes the negative sentence, retains only the valid positive-control
   refinement, and is the sole revision submitted to `main`.

## Preconditions

Before the probe, an operator confirms the configured `gerrit` remote targets rebar's Gerrit and
that the deployed review bot posts `LLM-Review` results for this repository. Record the remote URL,
change id, patchset SHA, UTC timestamp, and bot vote/message on this ticket. If the bot is absent or
misconfigured, do not substitute an offline run: create an operational bug linked
`discovered_from`, mark this story blocked, and resume only after the live prerequisite is restored.

For this story, an acceptable review trace is the review sidecar JSON, either linked from the Gerrit
bot message or pasted into a ticket comment. It must contain the reviewed revision SHA, show the
physical id `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` in the executed-criteria coverage/batch
plan, and retain the `blocking`, `advisory`, `dropped`, and `indeterminate` verdict lists. Because
that physical id is project-only and absent from packaged built-ins, its execution against the named
revision is the observable proxy for repository-local prompt resolution; no unavailable prompt-path
or per-criterion forwarding-count field is required.

## Scope

- Keep `blocking_enabled: false` for the entire probe. `block_threshold: 0.90` is recorded but
  inert; no change in this story enables blocking.
- Record Gerrit change id, each patchset SHA, UTC timestamp, vote/message, project criterion finding,
  Pass-2 validity/impact attributes, Pass-3 priority/decision/threshold/posture, and final amended
  patchset outcome as ticket comments.
- Confirm the project-only physical prompt id ran against the reviewed repository SHA using the
  acceptable sidecar trace.
- Leave the repository with only valid review instructions. Threshold calibration and a future
  `blocking_enabled: true` decision require a separate ticket backed by accumulated real-review
  data; this story neither selects a lower threshold nor promotes the criterion.

This story is observational: it does not change the rubric in response to probe results. A rubric
or seam defect discovered by the probe is captured as a new ticket linked `discovered_from` and is
fixed only through that separately reviewed work.

If the pinned negative directive does not produce a criterion finding, record the unexpected trace,
create a rubric/seam bug linked `discovered_from`, mark this story blocked, and do not improvise a
different probe within this ticket.

After the final patchset, post a calibration handoff comment on this ticket with the exact labels
`Priority distribution:` and `Blocking promotion: deferred`; the latter must name a future,
separately reviewed, data-backed posture ticket as the only path to enabling blocking.

Out of scope: changing the rubric, collecting multi-day adoption metrics, changing the impact
model, or enabling blocking.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] [operator-attested] The Gerrit remote and review-bot precondition is recorded with remote URL,
  change id, patchset SHA, UTC timestamp, and bot vote/message before either probe is evaluated.
- [ ] Before and after both probes, project routing remains `block_threshold: 0.90` and
  `blocking_enabled: false`; verify with
  `.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False'`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] A positive-control Gerrit patchset is recorded with change id, patchset
  SHA, UTC timestamp, and an acceptable sidecar trace proving the project-only physical criterion ran
  against that revision and emitted no phase-boundary outcome for a valid discovery/evidence
  refinement. No entry in `blocking`, `advisory`, `dropped`, or `indeterminate` names
  `project.review-phase-boundaries`; entries from unrelated criteria do not affect this assertion.
- [ ] [operator-attested] A negative-probe Gerrit patchset is recorded with change id, patchset SHA,
  UTC timestamp, the pinned severity test sentence, and an acceptable sidecar trace. The trace shows
  the project-only physical id ran, the grounded `project.review-phase-boundaries` finding appears in
  `advisory`, its verification contains Pass-2 validity/impact answers, and its deterministic Pass-3
  fields show `decision: advisory`, threshold `0.90`, and `blocking_enabled: false`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The negative directive is absent from the final submitted patchset, the
  project criterion emits no phase-boundary finding on that revision, and the recorded final
  revision contains only phase-compliant instructions. This third patchset of the same Gerrit change
  is the only probe revision submitted to `main`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] A ticket comment contains the exact labels `Priority distribution:` with
  the observed Pass-3 values and `Blocking promotion: deferred` with an explicit handoff to a future,
  separately reviewed, data-backed posture ticket.
- [ ] The final cleaned dogfood change is landed on `main`; verify with
  `git log origin/main --oneline --grep='rebar-ticket: 9447-b31c-e0b7-446d'`.

## Dependencies

Depends on both the generic integration-seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` (including its child bug
`8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`) and the project-criterion authoring story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b`.
